result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: \sqrt{1 + \frac{a_{dS}^2}{a^2}} 
JUSTIFICATION: In the context of de Sitter-modified Unruh temperature, the effective acceleration is the quadrature sum of the local acceleration 'a' and the de Sitter acceleration 'a_{dS}', leading to the ratio of the temperatures being the square root of the sum of their squared accelerations divided by the local acceleration.
